title: BOTHROS: Aegean sign reading from photographs (Linear A + Linear B)
description:
A two-stage computer-vision pipeline that reads signs on photographs and facsimiles of Linear A and Linear B tablets: a single unified Aegean sign detector (YOLO11) localises signs for both scripts, and a per-script ConvNeXt-Tiny classifier identifies each by catalogue code (AB-codes for Linear A, B-codes for Linear B) and phonetic reading where known.

This archive is the inference source code (MIT). Trained model weights are hosted separately on the Hugging Face Hub under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (https://huggingface.co/JMacD263/linear-a-linear-b-bothros), as they are derived from research-only corpora (GORILA, DĀMOS, SigLA).

All reported results are leak-free and on held-out test tablets (tablet-ID-level split enforced at crop-extraction and detector-training time). The system is benchmarked against the DĀMOS (Linear B) and SigLA (Linear A) corpora and compared to the DeepScribe cuneiform reference.
notes: This archive is the inference SOURCE CODE only, licensed MIT. The trained model weights are NOT in this deposit; they are hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial, derived from research-only corpora). Cite the GitHub repository and the weights repository separately.
